steps.title: Trace the structure
steps.content: The text first says Nathan Alder is not as well known as major American writers. It then names two influential champions of his work, Aaron Fields and Martin Vale, before explaining Vale's reason for admiring Little, Big.

steps.title: Match all three moves
steps.content: A turns limited fame into an explanation, B invents a successful career, and D claims the work is difficult and made more accessible. The structure is limited recognition, positive opinions, and one supporter's reasoning. The answer is C.
